subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand what an ecosystem is and its basic parts
Learn simple food chains and how energy moves in nature
Know the difference between biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste
Understand why the ozone layer is important and what harms it
Recognize how humans affect the environment and why we should care
Enjoy learning about nature through pictures, lists, and easy activities

lesson_plan.title: What is an Ecosystem?
teaching_strategy: Show pictures or draw simple ecosystem on board
Ask students to name things they see in a garden/pond
student_activities: Draw a simple garden or pond ecosystem
List 5 living things and 5 non-living things in it

lesson_plan.title: Food Chain – Who Eats Whom?
teaching_strategy: Tell simple story examples (grass → rabbit → fox)
student_activities: Draw 3 simple food chains (e.g., grass → deer → tiger)
Color the plants green, animals accordingly

lesson_plan.title: Waste – What Happens to It?
topics_covered: Biodegradable (rots easily) vs non-biodegradable (does not rot)
Examples: banana peel vs plastic bag
Why we should not throw plastic everywhere
teaching_strategy: Show real items (paper, peel, plastic, bottle)
student_activities: Make two lists: biodegradable and non-biodegradable (10 items each)
Draw happy earth for biodegradable and sad earth for plastic
Share one idea to reduce plastic
assessment: Fun drawings and one good idea

lesson_plan.title: Ozone Layer – Our Sun Shield
topics_covered: Ozone protects us from bad sun rays
CFCs (from old sprays, fridges) harm ozone
teaching_strategy: Draw Earth with umbrella (ozone) and sun rays
Show before-after picture of ozone hole
student_activities: Draw Earth with ozone layer as shield
Color harmful rays red and good rays yellow
Write 2 ways to help ozone (e.g., no extra sprays)

lesson_plan.title: We and Our Environment
topics_covered: How people harm nature (too much waste, cutting trees)
How to help (plant trees, save water, reuse)
teaching_strategy: Show happy vs sad nature pictures
student_activities: Draw one way they can help nature (planting, not littering)
Write or say “I promise to…” sentence

lesson_plan.title: Fun Revision and Quiz Game
topics_covered: Easy questions on ecosystem, food chain, waste, ozone
Why we love and protect nature
teaching_strategy: Big colorful mind map on board
Fun quiz game (hands up, team points)
student_activities: Answer simple quiz questions (oral or show fingers)
Add one drawing or word to class mind map
Say one thing they learned and liked

teaching_aids: Colorful charts/pictures of ecosystems, food chains, waste items
Real waste samples (safe ones: paper, peel, plastic piece)
Stickers or smiley stamps for encouragement
teacher_tip: Keep everything light and positive. Use lots of pictures, colors, and stories. Praise every effort. Connect to students’ daily life (home waste, school garden, seeing plastic in streets). Make students feel they can really help nature.
